name: Ben
description: BEN is the protagonist of THE WAIF. A father and mechanic by trade, he is trapped in a recursive cycle of death and rebirth spanning multiple timelines, bound by quantum entanglement to [Waif](waif.md). His story is driven by overwhelming guilt over the death of his son, [Jack](jack.md), and the dissolution of his marriage to [Marie](marie.md). Ben struggles with chronic insomnia, haunted by fragm

name: Jack
description: JACK is the six-year-old son of [Ben](ben.md) and [Marie](marie.md). He dies in a car accident early in the story — a jumper ([Waif](waif.md) in a previous life, wearing red-striped sneakers) crashes through the roof of the family's Subaru Forester at an intersection. His death is not merely a tragic backstory: it is the traumatic event that anchors the entire recursive loop.

name: Jamie
description: JAMIE is the owner of Last Chance Supply, a hardware and general store in Broken Bow. He is a supporting character who provides [Ben](ben.md) with ammunition — a transaction that, in context, is not merely practical but quietly ominous. Jamie also offers Ben bootleg alcohol, operating in the margins of legality and propriety that characterize a small-town merchant who knows his customers and their

name: Joyce Preston
description: JOYCE PRESTON is the partner of [Querry Preston](querry-preston.md) and co-resident of the mansion that [Ben](ben.md) and [Waif](waif.md) robbed in a previous timeline. During the break-in, Waif struck her with a cosh — a blunt-force weapon — incapacitating her while Ben confronted Querry. Joyce was present when Ben took the revolver and shot Querry dead, making her a witness to the founding traum

name: Marie
description: MARIE is [Ben's](ben.md) ex-wife. A mathematician or academic by profession, she is practical, direct, and grounded — a stark contrast to the supernatural chaos that engulfs Ben. She and Ben lost their son, [Jack](jack.md), in a car accident that shattered their family. Marie remained in the suburban family home, living among the painful memories, while Ben retreated to the cabin. She has since re

name: Querry Preston
description: QUERRY PRESTON (Q.R. Preston) is the author of *The Rules of Quantum Immortality*, a book that articulates the metaphysical framework within which the story operates. He lived in a mansion with his partner, [Joyce Preston](joyce-preston.md), where he kept a revolver sealed in a rosewood box — the same revolver that [Ben](ben.md) later discovers at the cabin.

name: Ricky
description: RICKY is the sixteen-year-old son of [Jamie](jamie.md), owner of Last Chance Supply in Broken Bow. He uses a wheelchair and appears only briefly in the screenplay — a single image, seated in the hardware store, staring at a television screen. He has no dialogue, no action, no apparent agency. Yet his presence resonates with the story's deeper concerns: observation, stasis, and the relationship bet

name: Schrödinger
description: SCHRÖDINGER is a black cat that haunts the cabin. She presses against the windows, wants to get inside, and [Ben](ben.md) feeds her catfish catfood but refuses to let her in. The cat is named after Erwin Schrödinger's famous thought experiment — a cat in a box that is simultaneously alive and dead until observed — and her presence in the story is explicitly symbolic. She is not merely a pet or a p

name: Terry
description: Terry is a minor antagonist encountered by [Ben](../characters/ben.md) in [Broken Bow](../locations/broken-bow.md). He operates in the alley between shops on the main street, dealing drugs or otherwise skirting the law. Terry lurks in the shadows with hands stuffed in his jacket, presenting an immediate sense of threat as Ben tries to navigate the town.

name: The Man
description: **Physical Appearance** — The Man appears exclusively in flashback and is primarily defined by silhouette and context rather than detailed features. He is an **older man** — presumably in his 50s or 60s, established enough to occupy a corner office or penthouse with a glass wall overlooking New York City. In his first appearance, he is a **dark, backlit figure** — his face obscured by shadow, his

name: The Mother
description: **Physical Appearance** — The Mother appears as a woman in her 50s with wild, greasy hair that hangs over her face. She has a solid, formidable build — not tall but dense and unnaturally strong. Her face, when visible through her hair, has a weathered quality; her mouth can open *impossibly wide*, far beyond human jaw anatomy. Her hands are the primary instrument of her violence — strong enough to

name: The Others
description: **Physical Appearance** — The Others are **pale, ethereal figures** — their faces are blank, featureless or nearly so, without recognizable human expression. They are lit by **pale fire** — a cold, blue light that illuminates their forms from within or from the ambient supernatural flames that surround them. They are described as **hollow husks** — their bodies contain no substance. When a bullet

name: Woman in White
description: **Physical Appearance** — The Woman in White is a pale, ethereal figure — feminine in shape but profoundly unnatural in form and function. Her most distinctive feature is her **feet**: grimy, bare, with prominent **bony ankles** that trail the hem of her white dress. Her hands are **wiry** — thin, strong, skeletal — and her fingers can extend into **sharp elongated razor-like implements** capable

name: The Waif
description: **Physical Appearance** — Waif is a wiry, thin woman — "skin and bones" — roughly the same build and size as Ben's ex-wife Marie. She is perpetually dirty and disheveled throughout much of the story: her skin is visibly grimy, her fingers stained, her feet filthy with black-painted toenails and scratches running up her legs. Her eyes are dark-circled and haunted, often described as dark-eyed or de

name: The Cabin
description: Ben and Marie's family cabin in Broken Bow, Upstate New York — a cedar-sided, isolated structure surrounded by encroaching forest. The cabin serves as the primary setting for the screenplay's third act and is the site of the final, recursive confrontation where the loop is ultimately broken. It is both a place of childhood memory and the container of dark secrets, housing the rosewood box beneath

name: The Suburban House
description: Ben and Marie's primary residence — a middle-class suburban home on a tree-lined street in a New York State suburb. The house sits on a block with adjacent houses visible on either side, picket fences, and well-kept lawns — the picture of American suburban domesticity. Its interior is "middle-class decor buried under the perma-mess of a small child" (p. 2), with toys scattered across rooms, breakf

name: The Intersection
description: A busy crossroads where the car accident occurs — and where the loop both begins and ends. The intersection is the screenplay's central metaphor: a Schrödinger's cat box existing in superimposed states, where both death and survival coexist simultaneously depending on the observer's timeline branch. Marie's line — "I hate this intersection. We'd have been trapped there for five minutes" (p. 16) —

name: Broken Bow
description: A small lakeside town in Upstate New York that serves as the gateway to [the cabin](the-cabin.md) and the setting for Ben's uneasy integration into the local community during Act One. Broken Bow is a town of surfaces — a quaint main street masking a seedy underbelly, a place where both the beauty of the natural world and the corruption beneath it coexist in uneasy tension.

name: Preston Mansion
description: The Preston Mansion is the setting of Ben and Waif's shared criminal past — a grand house or estate associated with Querry Preston. It appears primarily in flashback during the Waif's revelations about their prior life together in [New York](new-york-city.md). The mansion is a space of wealth, secrecy, and the origin of the loop's criminal entanglement.

name: New York City
description: The urban backdrop for Ben and Waif's criminal past, the setting of the Preston mansion robbery, and the location of the court proceedings that formalize Ben and Marie's divorce. New York City exists primarily in flashback and implication — a world of skyscrapers, busy avenues, and corner offices that the present-tense narrative has left behind but can never fully escape. It is the origin point of

name: New York Upstate
description: The upstate New York sequences involve Ben (and JRM, the actor playing Ben) travelling between [Broken Bow](broken-bow.md), [the cabin](the-cabin.md), and the [suburban house](the-suburban-house.md). These sequences require car tracking shots, drone shots, and potentially mounted driving footage.

name: The Revolver
description: The revolver is the screenplay's central physical object — a strange heptagonal (seven-sided) firearm with six empty chambers and a sealed seventh chamber containing a radioactive element. It is discovered in an ornate rosewood box hidden in the cabin crawlspace, crushing a telephone wire against a wooden support — as if it were placed there deliberately, waiting to be found (p. 13). The weapon is

name: Red Striped Sneakers
description: The red-striped sneakers are the screenplay's most important visual motif — the object that connects [Waif](../characters/waif.md) to the accident that killed [Jack](../characters/jack.md) and destroyed [Ben](../characters/ben.md)'s family. The sneakers appear three times across the narrative, each appearance a breadcrumb leading to the climactic revelation that Waif is the jumper who caused the c

name: Pale Blue Fire
description: Pale blue fire is the visual signature of [the Others](../characters/the-others.md) — a cold, ethereal blue flame that lights their presence throughout the screenplay. It is not ordinary fire: it emits no heat, casts no shadow, and exists at the boundary between the physical world and whatever dimension the Others inhabit. The flame is their herald, their aura, and their weapon.
